title: 4-DOF Robotic Arm Controller
connections: PCA9685 SCL/SDA → Arduino A5/A4
PCA9685 V+ & GND → 5V 4A Supply (+/-)
Servos (Base, Shoulder, Elbow, Gripper) → PCA9685 Channels 0-3
Potentiometers outputs → Arduino A0-A3
tips: PCA9685 handles servo PWM signals locally over I2C, freeing the MCU from running software PWM interrupts.
Always supply power to PCA9685 servo channels from a dedicated external battery/adapter; microcontrollers cannot supply the startup current of 4 motors.

title: Bluetooth Remote Controlled Car
connections: HC-05 TXD → Arduino RXD (D0), RXD → TXD (via divider)
Motor Driver IN1-IN4 → Arduino D5, D6, D9, D10 (PWM enabled)
Driver OUT1/OUT2 → Left Motor, OUT3/OUT4 → Right Motor
7.4V Battery (+) → L298N 12V Pin, GND → Common GND
tips: PWM speed adjustments (analogWrite) allow smooth acceleration and decelerations, protecting gearboxes from sudden shock.
The HC-05 enters AT configuration mode when the onboard button is held down at power-up.

title: Hand Gesture Controlled Robot
connections: Glove: MPU6050 SDA/SCL → D2/D3 (or standard I2C), HC-05 TX/RX to serial pins
Robot car: HC-05 Bluetooth linked as Receiver, OUT1-OUT4 to motors
tips: Convert accelerometer raw axis values into pitch and roll tilt angles using trignometric arctan formulas.
Adjust transceiver baud rates to 9600 to minimize data dropouts over 10m ranges.

title: Maze Solver (Wall Follower)
connections: Front Ultrasonic Trig/Echo → D2/D3
Left Ultrasonic Trig/Echo → D4/D5
Right Ultrasonic Trig/Echo → D6/D7
tips: Implement a PID loop based on left/right distance readings to keep the robot aligned down the center of the path.
Ultrasonic sensor cross-talk can trigger false measurements. Introduce a short delay (e.g., 10-20ms) between triggers.

title: Warehouse RFID Cargo Rover
connections: RFID SDA/RST → D10/D9, standard SPI pins
Line sensors → A0, A1, A2
Relay / Gripper Servos → Servo Shield pins
tips: RFID cards use passive magnetic induction; tags must pass within 3cm of the reader antenna to trigger a read.
Integrate encoders to verify precise forward movements in case tape lines are damaged.

title: Fire Fighting Autonomous Rover
connections: Flame Sensors Digital Out (Left, Center, Right) → D2, D3, D4
Water Pump Relay Control → D13
Motor outputs → L298N driver
tips: Flame sensors sense infrared emissions (760nm - 1100nm). Incandescent lamps will cause false triggers; shield sensor faces.
Isolate the water container and hose to prevent leaks from shorting motor electronics.
